The chi-square statistic assessing the association between histological types and CDH1 gene mutations in female BRCA patients from the PanCancer Atlas is approximately **325.34**.

Details:
- Total patients included (after filtering): 762
- Categories retained (row marginal totals > 10): Infiltrating Ductal Carcinoma (549), Infiltrating Lobular Carcinoma (142), Other specify (36), Mixed Histology (22), Mucinous Carcinoma (13)
- Categories excluded (row marginal totals ≤ 10): Medullary Carcinoma (6), Metaplastic Carcinoma (6), Infiltrating Carcinoma NOS (1)
- CDH1 mutated column total: 99; CDH1 not mutated column total: 663
- Only reliable mutation entries (FILTER = 'PASS') were considered.

The dominant contribution comes from Infiltrating Lobular Carcinoma, which shows a very strong association with CDH1 mutations (83 observed vs 18.45 expected), consistent with the known biology of lobular breast cancer.

The chi-square statistic assessing the association between histological types and CDH1 gene mutations in female BRCA patients from the PanCancer Atlas is **364.17**.

Details:
- Analysis included 984 female BRCA patients with known histological types who had reliable (PASS filter) mutation data.
- 5 histological types were retained (marginal totals > 10): Infiltrating Ductal Carcinoma (736), Infiltrating Lobular Carcinoma (164), Other specify (44), Mixed Histology (27), and Mucinous Carcinoma (13).
- 3 histological types were excluded (marginal totals ≤ 10): Medullary Carcinoma (6), Metaplastic Carcinoma (6), and Infiltrating Carcinoma NOS (1).
- 99 patients had CDH1 mutations; 885 did not.
- The large chi-square value is driven primarily by Infiltrating Lobular Carcinoma, which shows a much higher rate of CDH1 mutations (83/164) compared to other types, especially Infiltrating Ductal Carcinoma (9/736).

The chi-square statistic assessing the association between histological types and the presence of CDH1 gene mutations in female BRCA patients from the PanCancer Atlas is approximately 325.34.

Details:
- After filtering to female BRCA patients with known histological types and reliable mutation entries (FILTER = 'PASS'), 762 patients remained across 5 histological type categories (excluding categories with marginal totals ≤ 10).
- Categories included: Infiltrating Ductal Carcinoma (549), Infiltrating Lobular Carcinoma (142), Other specify (36), Mixed Histology (22), and Mucinous Carcinoma (13).
- Categories excluded (row total ≤ 10): Infiltrating Carcinoma NOS (1), Medullary Carcinoma (6), Metaplastic Carcinoma (6).
- Column totals: CDH1 mutated = 99, No CDH1 mutation = 663.
- The very large chi-square value (325.34) indicates a highly significant association, primarily driven by the strong enrichment of CDH1 mutations in Infiltrating Lobular Carcinoma (83 observed vs 18.45 expected) and depletion in Infiltrating Ductal Carcinoma (9 observed vs 71.33 expected).
